title: alchemilla alpina subsp. conjuncta
intro: Alchemilla alpina subsp. conjuncta, also known as lady's mantle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) that is commonly found in mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Alchemilla alpina subsp. conjuncta include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and alleviate skin irritations. Alchemilla alpina subsp. conjuncta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, making it a versatile ingredient in natural remedies.
